sources.notes: Choose compatible particles maximizing total energy; answer each shop particle query.
Official solution sweeps possible chosen counts downward and maintains largest eligible energies.
Cross-check for top-k sum view and persistent segment tree variant.
Cross-check for heap/top-k maintenance and shop-particle replacement.

annotation.summary: Preprocess Bessie's particles so each shop particle query can return the best total energy when that extra particle is available.
annotation.constraints: Sum n and sum m are each <= 2e5; preprocessing all possible reactivity bounds and answering each query in O(1) is intended.
annotation.core_idea: For a fixed chosen count k without a shop particle, every chosen particle must have y >= k - 1, and the optimal subset is the k largest energies among eligible particles. Sweep k downward, insert newly eligible particles, and maintain the top energies. Also precompute the best value when a shop particle with reactivity y is forced in by allowing at most y original particles and replacing the weakest chosen original particle if needed.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n + m) per test case.
tricks: maintain top energies by reactivity threshold

solution_variants.name: decreasing-k-top-set
solution_variants.summary: Sort particles by reactivity, sweep the allowed selected count downward, keep the largest eligible energies in a heap/multiset, and answer shop queries from prefix maxima.

solution_variants.name: persistent-segment-tree-top-k
solution_variants.summary: Sort by reactivity and use persistent segment trees over energy values to query top-k sums for each count.

tags.evidence: For any fixed number of selected particles, choosing the largest energies among eligible particles is optimal.
The sweep maintains current top eligible energies and removes the smallest selected value when capacity shrinks.
All y thresholds are preprocessed before answering independent shop-particle queries.
Particles are processed in sorted reactivity order so eligibility changes monotonically.
